title: 10 best herbal decoctions for ear blockage
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for ear blockage are a natural remedy used to alleviate congestion, discomfort, and pressure in the ears. These concoctions are made by steeping herbs such as garlic, ginger, and peppermint in hot water to create a soothing liquid that can be poured into the ear canal. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of these herbs help to reduce swelling and eliminate excess wax, promoting drainage and relieving ear blockage. For example, drinking a cup of tea made from equal parts of garlic, ginger, and peppermint before applying it to the ear may help alleviate ear congestion, allowing individuals to focus on daily tasks without distraction or discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein decoctions helps with ear blockage because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The decoction soothes irritated ears, reduces swelling, and breaks down excess wax, allowing for a clearer path for sound to pass through. Additionally, mullein's mucilaginous texture coats the ear canal, protecting it from further irritation and infection. As a result, mullein decoctions provide effective relief from ear blockage, discomfort, and congestion, promoting healthy hearing and overall ear well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Mullein's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and congestion in the ear canal, allowing for easier drainage of excess wax and debris.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Mullein's antimicrobial properties help combat infections caused by bacteria, viruses, or fungi that can contribute to ear blockage, promoting a healthy ear environment.
Expectorant properties: Mullein's expectorant properties help loosen and clear excess mucus and debris from the ear canal, making it easier for the body to expel them and reducing blockages.
remedy_parts: Roots: Mullein roots are a popular choice for ear decoctions due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help soothe and clear up ear infections.
Leaves: The leaves of the mullein plant are used in ear decoctions to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with ear blockage, as well as to provide natural antibiotic benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried verbascum thapsus flowers and leaves and rinse them thoroughly with cold water.
Combine the rinsed herbs with 2 cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass measuring cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 15 minutes, then strain it using a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean container.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey and 5 drops of tea tree oil to the cooled decoction and stir well.
Use this herbal decoction as ear drops by placing 2-3 drops in each affected ear canal two times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with ear blockage because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and reduce swelling in the Eustachian tube. This tube connects the middle ear to the back of the throat and plays a crucial role in equalizing air pressure and draining excess mucus. By reducing inflammation, licorice root decoctions help restore normal functioning of the Eustachian tube, allowing for easy drainage of earwax and relief from clogged ears. Regular use of herbal licorice decoctions can also help prevent recurring ear blockages.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Licorice root's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, reduce swelling and inflammation in the Eustachian tube, which helps relieve ear blockage and discomfort.

Expectorant property: The saponins present in licorice root help loosen and clear mucus and other debris from the ears, sinuses, and respiratory tract, making it easier to breathe and reducing feelings of blockage.
Mucolytic property: Licorice root's mucolytic compounds break down thickened or excess mucus, allowing for easier drainage and reduction of congestion in the Eustachian tube and ear canal.
remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with ear blockage.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for treating ear congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and transfer it to a clean glass container.
Add 250 milliliters of distilled water to the container, stirring occasionally, for 30 minutes at room temperature.
Decant the liquid into a separate glass container, leaving behind the solids and sediment.
Boil the decanted liquid in a stainless steel pot for 15-20 minutes, reducing it to approximately 100 milliliters.
Allow the decoction to cool to lukewarm before straining it through a clean cotton cloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower decoctions helps with ear blockage because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and ease congestion in the Eustachian tube. The decoction's antibacterial agents also help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the blockage. Additionally, purple coneflower's soothing compounds calm irritated tissues and promote healthy drainage, allowing the ears to function properly and alleviate feelings of pressure and discomfort. By promoting natural healing and reducing inflammation, purple coneflower decoctions provide a holistic approach to treating ear blockage.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Purple coneflower decoction has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the ears, which can contribute to ear blockage. This property helps to alleviate discomfort and pressure associated with ear congestion.

Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial compounds present in purple coneflower decoction have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause ear infections, which may lead to ear blockage. By targeting the underlying infection, this property helps to address the root cause of the blockage.

Mucolytic property: The mucolytic compounds in purple coneflower decoction help to break down excess mucus and other debris that can accumulate in the ears, contributing to blockage. This property can aid in clearing out obstructions and promoting healthy ear function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and congestion in the ears, making it an effective ingredient for soothing ear blockages.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes contain antiseptic properties that help to combat infection and promote healing in the ears, making them a crucial part of medicinal decoctions for ear blockage.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one tablespoon of dried echinacea purpurea flowers per eight ounces of boiling water.
Combine the measured ingredients in a heat-resistant cup or teapot and let it steep for five to seven minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container to remove solids.
Add one teaspoon of honey to enhance flavor and potential therapeutic benefits before serving warm.
Gently pour the decoction into affected ear canal using an eye dropper or bulb syringe.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with ear blockage because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The decoction, made from the flowers of Calendula officinalis, has been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated ears. Its natural analgesic effects can help reduce pain and discomfort associated with ear blockages, while its antimicrobial properties can combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to the blockage. As a result, pot marigold decoctions can provide effective relief from ear congestion and promote healthy ear function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Calendula's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling in the Eustachian tube and ear canal, which can alleviate ear blockage and associated discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: The antibacterial and antifungal activity of calendula may help combat middle ear infections or secondary infections that can cause ear blockage by reducing bacterial and fungal loads.

Expectorant properties: Calendula's expectorant actions can help loosen and clear mucus and debris from the Eustachian tube and ear canal, which can aid in relieving ear blockage and promoting normal drainage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties in the roots help to reduce swelling and combat infection in the ear canal, alleviating blockage.
Barks: The barks contain a compound that helps to break down excess wax and debris in the ear canal, promoting drainage and relief from blockage.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-15 grams of dried calendula flowers and 500ml of boiling water for the infusion process.
Measure out one tablespoon of dried calendula flowers and add it to a clean glass jar or teapot.
Pour 250ml of boiling water over the calendula flowers, allowing it to steep for 10-15 minutes to release the active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid decoction.
Allow the decoction to cool before using it as an ear drop, gently dropping 2-3 drops of the warm liquid into the affected ear twice daily for 7-10 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese date decoctions helps with ear blockage because of its unique properties that help to loosen and clear out earwax, as well as reduce inflammation and congestion. The combination of herbs in the decoction, such as Dang Gui and Fu Ling, work together to promote blood circulation and ease tension in the ears, allowing for a clearer flow of fluids and reducing blockages. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and calm irritated ear tissues, providing long-lasting relief from ear blockage.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: The decoction's anti-inflammatory property helps to reduce swelling and relieve pressure in the Eustachian tube, allowing for easier drainage and alleviation of ear blockage symptoms.
Expectorant property: The expectorant properties of the herbs in the decoction help to loosen and clear out excess mucus and debris from the ears, promoting healthy drainage and reducing congestion.

Antibacterial property: The antibacterial properties of the herbs in the decoction help to combat infection and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to ear blockage and promote healing of the Eustachian tube.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, Chinese date roots help reduce inflammation and relieve ear congestion.
Rhyzomes: With its sweet and slightly bitter flavor, Chinese date rhyzome is believed to warm the channels, dispel cold, and relieve ear blockage caused by wind-cold invasion.
remedy_recipe: Collect 30 grams of dried ziziphus jujuba fruits and rinse them with pure water.
Boil the rinsed ziziphus jujuba fruits in 600 milliliters of distilled water for 45 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding solids.
Allow the strained decoction to cool down to room temperature and store it in an airtight container.
Use 10-15 milliliters of the cooled ziziphus jujuba decoction three times daily by pouring it into the affected ear canal.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder decoctions helps with ear blockage because they possess potent antimicrobial properties that effectively combat infections and reduce inflammation in the ear canal. The decongestant qualities of elder flowers also help to relieve pressure and ease congestion, allowing for a clearer flow of fluids and a reduction in blockages. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of elder can soothe irritated tissues, reducing discomfort and promoting healing. By addressing these underlying issues, elder decoctions can provide relief from ear blockage and promote overall ear health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Elder decoctions have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which helps to combat bacterial or fungal infections that can cause ear blockage and inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in elder flowers and berries have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the ears, making it easier to drain fluids and relieve blockages.

Expectorant action: Elder decoctions contain expectorants that help loosen and clear mucus and phlegm from the respiratory tract, including the Eustachian tube, which connects the middle ear to the back of the throat and helps regulate ear pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots have been traditionally used to treat ear infections and ear blockages due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome, or underground stem, of the elder plant contains compounds that help reduce inflammation and promote drainage in the ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra flowers at dawn when they are most potent for better results.
Combine one tablespoon of dried sambucus nigra flowers or two tablespoons of fresh flowers with one cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture in a saucepan for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, discarding the solids after use.
Store the cooled decoction in an amber glass bottle in the refrigerator and consume within 24 hours as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with ear blockage because its antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties help to reduce swelling and soothe irritated ears. The decoction's natural diuretic properties also aid in draining excess fluids and mucus from the ear canal, relieving pressure and congestion. Additionally, dandelion's ability to stimulate blood flow and promote lymphatic drainage helps to clear out impurities and toxins that may be contributing to blockage. This natural remedy can provide effective relief for individuals experiencing ear blockages due to sinus infections, allergies, or other conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxanthin, help reduce inflammation in the ears, which can contribute to blockages and discomfort.
Antimicrobial properties: Dandelion contains antimicrobial compounds like flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause ear infections and blockages.
Mucolytic properties: Dandelion's mucolytic enzymes help break down and clear out excess mucus and debris in the ears, which can contribute to blockage and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dandelion roots contain a high concentration of inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps reduce inflammation and promotes healing in the ears.
Flowers: The flowers of the dandelion plant are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm irritated ear tissues.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried taraxacum officinale leaves per 250 milliliters of boiling water.
Combine the measured taraxacum officinale leaves and boiling water in a saucepan and let steep for five minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup, discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool until it reaches a comfortable temperature suitable for ear drops, taking about ten minutes.
Use the cooled taraxacum officinale decoction as ear drops by filling the ear canal with the liquid and tilting the head to allow drainage, repeating up to three times daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle decoctions helps with ear blockage because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The decoction's bioactive compounds, such as caffeic acid and quercetin, have been shown to reduce swelling in the Eustachian tube, allowing for easier drainage of excess mucus and debris that can cause blockages. remedy_recipe: Rinse dried nettle leaves thoroughly with filtered water to remove impurities.
Combine the reserved nettle decoction with equal parts of warm water.
Gently pour the cooled nettle solution into a clean ear canal.
Repeat the administration of nettle decoctions for at least 7-10 days.
Store any remaining nettle decoction in an amber glass bottle with a tight-fitting cap.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort decoctions helps with ear blockage because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated eardrums and reduce swelling. The decoction's antimicrobial qualities also help combat infections that can cause blockages, allowing for a clearer flow of earwax and debris to exit the ear canal. remedy_properties: Antibacterial and Antifungal Properties: St John's Wort contains compounds like hypericin and hyperforin that exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activities, which helps to combat infections in the ear canal that may be causing blockage.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The flavonoids present in St John's Wort decoctions have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and relieve congestion in the ear canal, making it easier for mucus and debris to drain out and restore hearing.

Antioxidant Properties: The antioxidants in St John's Wort decoctions, such as hyperoxin and pseudohypericin, help protect the ear tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic ear blockage and related symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort contain bioactive compounds like hyperforin, which can help reduce inflammation and ease pain in the ears.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st john's wort flowers and leaves and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine one tablespoon of dried or two tablespoons of fresh herbs with one cup of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar and discard the solids.
Store the final decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to three days or freeze for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow decoctions helps with ear blockage because of its natural antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. When used as a warm compress or ear drops, yarrow decoction can help to dissolve excess wax, reduce swelling, and promote drainage in the Eustachian tube. Barks: The barks of the yarrow plant contain flavonoids, which have natural antibacterial and antiviral properties that can help combat infections causing ear blockage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in the morning, ensuring they are dry and free of contaminants.
Rinse 20-30 grams of harvested material gently with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine rinsed plant material with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a large glass jar, allowing it to steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid decoction.
Store the cooled decoction in an amber glass bottle, keeping it refrigerated for up to three days before use as ear drops.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary decoctions helps with ear blockage because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's essential oils, such as camphor and borneol, help to reduce swelling and congestion in the Eustachian tube, allowing for easier drainage and relief from pressure buildup. Antioxidant properties: Rosemary's antioxidants, such as carnosic acid, help protect the ear canal from oxidative stress and damage caused by environmental toxins and other irritants that can contribute to ear blockage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and alleviating discomfort caused by ear blockages.
Barks: The barks of rosemary contain antioxidants and antibacterial compounds that help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection in the ear canal.
remedy_recipe: Measure two teaspoons of dried rosemary leaves and one teaspoon of dried echinacea root.
Combine the measured herbs with four cups of boiling distilled water in a heat-resistant glass or ceramic container.
Reduce the mixture's temperature to a simmer for 10-15 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature and store it in an airtight container for up to 24 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint decoctions helps with ear blockage because its menthol content increases blood flow to the ears, relieving congestion and pressure. The anti-inflammatory properties of peppermint also reduce swelling in the Eustachian tube, allowing for easier drainage of mucus and reducing the sensation of fullness or plugged ears. Additionally, the warm temperature of the decoction helps to relax the muscles around the ear canal, making it easier to pop the eardrum and restore normal hearing function.
remedy_properties: Menthol content: Peppermint's high menthol content helps to thin mucus and reduce congestion in the ears, making it easier to clear out debris and reduce blockages.

Barks: The barks of the peppermint plant contain antioxidants that help to soothe and calm the skin around the ears, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Measure 2 tablespoons of dried peppermint leaves into a heat-resistant glass measuring cup.
Heat 8 ounces of distilled water in a saucepan over medium-high heat until it reaches boiling point.
Add the measured peppermint leaves to the boiling water and reduce heat to low simmer for 5 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let the mixture steep for an additional 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer into a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey decoctions helps with ear blockage because of its unique properties. The allantoin present in comfrey has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort caused by ear congestion. Additionally, the antioxidants and mucilages found in comfrey can help to break down and dissolve excess mucus and debris that may be blocking the eustachian tube, allowing for easy drainage and relief from ear blockage. remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried symphytum officinale herb or two teaspoons of fresh herb into a clean glass jar.
Heat one cup of distilled water in a saucepan over medium heat until it reaches a boil.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it steep for 5 to 10 minutes with the lid slightly askew.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool down to room temperature before storing it in the refrigerator for up to three days or freezing it for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel decoctions helps with ear blockage because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The volatile oils present in fennel, such as anethole and limonene, have been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate congestion in the Eustachian tube, which connects the middle ear to the back of the throat. remedy_recipe: Measure accurately one teaspoon of dried foeniculum vulgare herb or one tablespoon of fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves.
Combine measured foeniculum vulgare with one cup of distilled water in a saucepan.
Boil the mixture over medium heat for 10 to 15 minutes to release bioactive compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container discarding solids.
Allow the cooled decoction to steep at room temperature for 30 minutes before using as ear drops.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with ear blockage because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated tissues in the ear canal. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in sufficient quantity for the required amount of infusion.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried flowers or two teaspoons of fresh flowers per cup of boiling water.
Combine the measured herbs with boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into a separate container and discard the solids immediately.
Use the cooled decoction as ear drops after mixing it with a carrier oil like olive or coconut oil at a ratio of 1:3.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry decoctions helps with ear blockage because its unique combination of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds possesses potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These properties help to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the Eustachian tube, which is responsible for regulating air pressure in the middle ear. Seeds: Contain essential fatty acids that soothe and moisturize the inner ear canal, relieving blockages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried vaccinium macrocarpon berries in quantities of 2-3 tablespoons for every 8 ounces of water.
Rinse the harvested berries gently with clean water and pat them dry with a paper towel.
Combine the berries with 8 ounces of boiling water in a medium-sized saucepan and let it steep for 15-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids.
Let the decoction cool to room temperature then store it in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.

Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Discard the solids and let the decoction cool to room temperature taking about 30 minutes.
Use the cooled decoction as ear drops by pouring a few drops into the affected ear twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender decoctions helps with ear blockage because its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce swelling and ease congestion in the ears. The decoction's natural antibacterial compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, can also combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to the blockage. Additionally, lavender's calming effects can help to soothe discomfort and promote relaxation, making it an effective remedy for relieving ear pressure and discomfort associated with blockage.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Lavender's antibacterial properties can help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to ear blockage, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, can help reduce swelling and ease pain caused by ear blockages, making it a natural remedy for conditions like otitis media (middle ear infection).

Decongestant property: Lavender's decongestant properties can help alleviate excessive mucus production and congestion in the ears, which is often a symptom of ear blockage, allowing for easier drainage and relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of English lavender contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and ease ear congestion.
Buds: Lavender buds are rich in essential oils, particularly linalool, which has natural antibacterial properties that can help combat infections causing ear blockage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and store them in an airtight container.
Measure out 20ml of boiling water and pour it over the dried lavender flowers in a heat-resistant cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 15-20 minutes, releasing the essential oils and flavonoids from the lavender flowers.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids.
Use the cooled and filtered herbal decoction as ear drops, filling the ear canal with 2-3ml of the solution for 5-7 minutes before draining.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme decoctions helps with ear blockage because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. When made into a warm decoction, thyme's active compounds can be easily absorbed through the eardrum, reducing swelling and congestion caused by excess mucus or fluid buildup. This gentle yet effective remedy can help to clear out impacted earwax, ease discomfort, and promote healthy drainage, restoring normal hearing function and alleviating feelings of pressure and fullness in the ears.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial infections that can cause ear blockages, promoting a healthy ear canal environment.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory properties reduce swelling and irritation in the ear canal, helping to alleviate discomfort and congestion associated with ear blockages.
Decongestant properties: Thyme's decongestant properties help to loosen and clear out excess mucus and debris that can cause ear blockages, promoting drainage and relieving pressure.

Rhyzomes: The underground stems of the thyme plant, known as rhyzomes, have anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and ease congestion in the ears.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers, measuring approximately 10 grams per preparation.
Combine the thymus vulgaris ingredients with 500 milliliters of distilled water in a medium-sized saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce the temperature to low and simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before storing it in an amber glass bottle for future use.

remedy_recipe: Measure 10-15 grams of dried melissa officinalis flowers.
Combine the measured herbs with 250ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herb to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat ear blockage with herbal decoctions is to combine ingredients that promote earwax removal and reduction of inflammation. Mix equal parts of turmeric powder and ginger juice with warm water to create a soothing decoction. Strain the mixture and use an ear syringe or dropper to gently flush the ears twice daily, repeating as needed until symptoms subside.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ear blockage that are treated with herbal decoctions are various respiratory issues such as bronchitis, coughs, and congestion. Decoctions made from herbs like eucalyptus, thyme, and ginger help to loosen mucus and ease breathing, providing relief from symptoms. Additionally, herbal decoctions may be used to treat sinusitis, rhinitis, and pharyngitis, which also involve blockages or obstructions in the nasal passages and throat.
